jokes.text: hermano in Spanish meant brother, and the person Marta was infatuated with was, in fact, himself
jokes.setup: Michael discovers Marta is interested in someone named Hermano and confronts her about cheating
jokes.punchline: Hermano means brother - she was talking about Michael all along
jokes.explanation: Classic AD misdirection with linguistic misunderstanding. Well-structured reveal that recontextualizes everything we thought we knew.

jokes.text: See if you can get it down to five.
jokes.setup: Lawyer says they need to look like loving family for ten minutes
jokes.punchline: George immediately wants to reduce the time commitment to his own family
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing George's self-centeredness even in crisis

jokes.text: Oh, get off the mount. You're coming home, guilty or somehow found not guilty.
jokes.setup: George being dramatic about confessing
jokes.punchline: Lucille dismisses his martyrdom and adds darkly comic qualifier about his innocence
jokes.explanation: Sharp Lucille line with perfect dismissive tone and dark implication about his guilt

jokes.text: You're a good guy, mon frère. That means brother in French. I don't know why I know that. I took four years of Spanish.
jokes.setup: Gob calling Michael brother in French
jokes.punchline: Claims not to know why he knows French when he studied Spanish for four years
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob logic - showcasing his shallow pretentiousness and complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Oh, who cares? Nobody respects him.
jokes.setup: Marta apologizing for being disrespectful to his brother
jokes.punchline: Michael dismisses her concern by casually insulting Gob
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's conflicted feelings about Gob - trying to be noble but can't help himself

jokes.text: I also like your hair and your face and your breasts.
jokes.setup: Marta praising Michael's moral character
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with blunt physical attraction
jokes.explanation: Subverts romantic moment with hilariously blunt sexual appreciation

jokes.text: Gob is like the cock of the walk. But not you. You are so kind. You're sensitive, like a woman.
jokes.setup: Marta contrasting Michael favorably with Gob
jokes.punchline: Her compliment becomes increasingly unflattering to Michael's masculinity
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from compliment to backhanded insult, showing how her praise becomes emasculating

jokes.text: That's enough.
jokes.setup: Marta calling him sensitive like a woman
jokes.punchline: Michael's terse response shows he's reached his limit of her 'compliments'
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - economical response that shows Michael's wounded pride

jokes.text: He likes to make love to this.
jokes.setup: Gob's music starts playing during intimate moment
jokes.punchline: Marta reveals Gob's narcissistic sexual habits
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Gob's narcissism while creating maximum awkwardness for the moment

jokes.text: Not-Not-Not... Not... Not hot wrong. You know, regular wrong.
jokes.setup: Michael and Marta saying their attraction is wrong
jokes.punchline: Michael has to clarify they mean morally wrong, not sexually exciting wrong
jokes.explanation: Brilliant distinction between moral wrongness and sexual excitement, perfectly delivered stammering

jokes.text: While I'm doing my noble thing you could put those photos in a box, get rid of the wand... and we can do the wrong thing the right way.
jokes.setup: Michael planning how to handle the situation with Gob
jokes.punchline: Rationalizes their affair as doing 'the wrong thing the right way' while asking her to hide evidence
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay showing Michael's ability to rationalize anything, plus practical affair planning

jokes.text: Oh, great. And now you're mocking me, you selfish, country-music-loving lady.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's sarcastic response to his complaints
jokes.punchline: Tobias's oddly specific insult about country music
jokes.explanation: Tobias's hilariously specific and ineffective insult shows his poor conflict skills

jokes.text: Why don't you two just get a divorce and save us all a lot of grief?
jokes.setup: Parents fighting with transparent cover story
jokes.punchline: Maeby cuts through their BS with practical solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Maeby moment - cutting through adult nonsense with teenage directness

jokes.text: And then, of course, we couldn't conceive, so there was that famous ordeal.
jokes.setup: Tobias casually mentioning fertility issues
jokes.punchline: Calling conception problems a 'famous ordeal' with typical Tobias pomposity
jokes.explanation: Sets up major plot point while showing Tobias's tendency to dramatize everything

jokes.text: Sometimes I wish we weren't even related.
jokes.setup: George Michael asking about family relationships
jokes.punchline: Maeby unknowingly expresses George Michael's exact hope
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Maeby says exactly what George Michael wants to hear but for different reasons

jokes.text: Hey, you know, maybe we're not.
jokes.setup: Maeby wishing they weren't related
jokes.punchline: George Michael immediately seizes on the possibility with obvious ulterior motives
jokes.explanation: Shows George Michael's transparent desperation and questionable priorities

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Dozens of times.
jokes.setup: George Michael asking if Lindsay was ever pregnant
jokes.punchline: Gob's casual claim that she was pregnant dozens of times
jokes.explanation: Hilariously absurd response delivered with Gob's typical casual authority

jokes.text: Tell you what, you may not be good with women, but you are great with other people's women.
jokes.setup: Gob thanking Michael for relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about Michael being good at stealing girlfriends
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob line - manages to be grateful and insulting simultaneously with dramatic irony

jokes.text: I don't answer the phone, that doesn't happen.
jokes.setup: Gob explaining why he won't answer Marta's calls
jokes.punchline: His logic that avoiding bad news prevents it from being true
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob magical thinking - believes he can prevent reality through avoidance

jokes.text: And it's all because of you, fratello. That's Italian for brother.
jokes.setup: Earlier French 'mon frère' comment
jokes.punchline: Gob continues showing off language skills he clearly doesn't understand systematically
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag of Gob's pretentious foreign language use

jokes.text: Mom always taught us to curl up in a ball and remain motionless when confronted.
jokes.setup: Gob talking about using fists to fight
jokes.punchline: Buster learned anti-violence technique from his mother
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character detail showing Lucille's overprotective parenting and Buster's complete inability to be aggressive

jokes.text: I think that was about being attacked by bears.
jokes.setup: Buster explaining his anti-confrontation technique
jokes.punchline: Michael points out this advice was for bear attacks, not bullies
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response revealing the absurdity of Lucille's parenting advice

jokes.text: Yeah, I know.
jokes.setup: Michael pointing out the bear attack context
jokes.punchline: Buster acknowledges he knew this but used it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical punchline showing Buster's warped logic and acceptance of inappropriate advice

jokes.text: Hey, rich kid. Gimme your lunch money.
jokes.setup: Buster wondering about fighting
jokes.punchline: His attempt to roleplay a schoolyard bully with himself
jokes.explanation: Buster's pathetic attempt at tough guy roleplay, highlighting his sheltered upbringing.

jokes.text: You'd be a much bigger ball.
jokes.setup: Buster wondering about fighting today versus high school
jokes.punchline: Gob's cruel but logical observation about Buster's weight gain
jokes.explanation: Mean but funny callback to the ball position with Gob's typical cruelty

jokes.text: I want to dance. I want to make love to a woman. I want to get a checking account. I want to know what it feels like to get my face socked in.
jokes.setup: Buster talking about life experiences he's missed
jokes.punchline: Puts getting a checking account on same level as sex and violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Buster's sheltered life - mundane and exciting experiences equally foreign

jokes.text: I'm not here.
jokes.setup: Phone rings and might be Marta
jokes.punchline: Buster's immediate panic and denial of his own existence
jokes.explanation: Shows Buster's immediate retreat from any potential conflict or awkwardness

jokes.text: Tell him I'm fine without him.
jokes.setup: Buster hiding from mother's call
jokes.punchline: Lucille's transparent neediness disguised as independence
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille manipulation - claiming independence while desperately seeking attention

jokes.text: Where is your head, Michael?
jokes.setup: Michael confused about court time after being told it's Friday
jokes.punchline: Lucille questioning his focus when audience knows he's distracted by Marta
jokes.explanation: Ironic since audience knows exactly where Michael's head is - on Marta

jokes.text: We couldn't get that down, huh?
jokes.setup: Learning court appearance is 10 minutes
jokes.punchline: Callback to George Sr. wanting it reduced to 5 minutes
jokes.explanation: Mild callback showing Michael inherited his father's attitude about family time

jokes.text: Let's see if they've beefed up my part. I got three lines!
jokes.setup: Tobias excited about script rewrite
jokes.punchline: His enthusiasm for getting three whole lines, plus 'beefed up' wordplay
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment showing his delusions about his acting career, plus episode title reference

jokes.text: When a man needs to prove to a woman that he's actually... When a man loves a woman... and he actually wants to make love, uh, to her...
jokes.setup: George Michael asking about Maeby's conception
jokes.punchline: Tobias's incredibly awkward attempt at explaining sex
jokes.explanation: Painful cringe comedy as Tobias struggles with basic sex ed while talking to a teenager

jokes.text: I'm gonna stop you. I know where you're running.
jokes.setup: Tobias's painful explanation of sexual mechanics
jokes.punchline: George Michael mercifully cuts off the explanation
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man moment saving audience from more awkwardness

jokes.text: Tobias was a never-nude, which is exactly what it sounds like.
jokes.setup: Tobias mentioning nudity issues
jokes.punchline: Narrator's matter-of-fact introduction of completely absurd condition
jokes.explanation: Iconic AD moment - absurd concept delivered with perfect deadpan authority. Became cultural reference.

jokes.text: What do you think I wear these for?
jokes.setup: Explaining his never-nude condition
jokes.punchline: Reference to his jean shorts that he apparently never removes
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag setup - explains his constant cutoff shorts as medical necessity

jokes.text: I was never actually clear on that.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining why he wears cutoffs
jokes.punchline: George Michael's honest admission of confusion about his uncle's wardrobe
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response acknowledging the obvious weirdness everyone ignores

jokes.text: I don't know... weird brother of prime rib.
jokes.setup: Comparing himself to Michael in terms of attractiveness to women
jokes.punchline: Hilariously awkward meat metaphor for Michael's lesser appeal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insult showing Gob's crude thinking and inability to complete his own metaphor properly

jokes.text: Hermano means brother? Yeah. Well, sounds like hermano is about to get his ass kicked.
jokes.setup: Gob finally learning what hermano means
jokes.punchline: Still threatens to fight 'hermano' not realizing it's Michael
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony - audience knows he's threatening his own brother while he remains oblivious

jokes.text: Well, sounds like someone who you think's name is Tio... is about to get his ass kicked.
jokes.setup: Buster suggesting Tio as the rival
jokes.punchline: Gob's unnecessarily complex phrasing to acknowledge uncertainty while maintaining tough guy act
jokes.explanation: Funny over-qualification showing Gob trying to maintain authority while admitting ignorance

jokes.text: Mom wanted me to tell you... that she doesn't care whether you live or die, but if you're not dead... she would like to see you at the courthouse tomorrow in the blue sweater.
jokes.setup: Passing along message from Lucille
jokes.punchline: Casual death threat followed by specific fashion demands
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille message - combines emotional manipulation with maternal micromanagement

jokes.text: She's not real. She was made in a cup. Like soup... a hundred and thirty thousand dollar cup of soup.
jokes.setup: George Michael asking about Maeby's conception
jokes.punchline: Lucille's blunt description of artificial conception using soup metaphor with cost emphasis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Lucille moment - reduces miracle of life to financial transaction with absurd soup comparison

jokes.text: How do you like them egg rolls, Mr. Goldstone?
jokes.setup: Explaining Maeby's expensive conception
jokes.punchline: Random old-timey showbiz reference that makes no contextual sense
jokes.explanation: Weird Lucille non sequitur that feels like misplaced vaudeville reference

jokes.text: All I know is it took an extra year before we could add a den.
jokes.setup: Discussing the cost of Maeby's conception
jokes.punchline: Reducing the miracle of creating life to delaying home renovation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille priority system - home improvement versus grandchild's existence

jokes.text: You all right? Where am I? Am I in... two-thirds of a hospital room?
jokes.setup: Gob waking up after being knocked out
jokes.punchline: Immediately notices and quantifies the cheap soap opera set quality
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about cheap soap opera production values, precisely quantified

jokes.text: If you liked her, I would have said, 'Go for it.' I do kind of like her. Go for it.
jokes.setup: Gob saying he wouldn't mind if Michael liked Marta
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately takes him up on it with embarrassing directness
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's eagerness and lack of subtlety when getting what he wants

jokes.text: My brother said we can do it. That didn't come out as romantic as I'd hoped, but...
jokes.setup: Michael telling Marta they have permission to be together
jokes.punchline: Recognizing his own phrasing sounds more transactional than romantic
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment showing Michael's awkwardness in romantic situations

jokes.text: I understand you not wanting to do it on film... but you can't even get undressed in front of me.
jokes.setup: Tobias refusing to do nude scene
jokes.punchline: Lindsay revealing their marriage lacks basic physical intimacy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing depth of Tobias's issues affecting their marriage

jokes.text: I know for a fact that there are two members of German Parliament. They're called 'nein wohlstandig' nude.
jokes.setup: Defending his never-nude condition as real affliction
jokes.punchline: Claims German parliamentary representation for his made-up condition
jokes.explanation: Great Tobias fabrication with fake German to add false authority to absurd claim

jokes.text: Look, how 'bout we do this together? You take off your shirt, and I'll take off my jacket.
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to help Tobias overcome his nudity issues
jokes.punchline: Hilariously unequal trade offer showing she doesn't understand the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Lindsay's shallow understanding disguised as helpfulness

jokes.text: Now all I need is a boyfriend.
jokes.setup: George Michael deciding not to tell Maeby about adoption to preserve her family
jokes.punchline: Maeby unknowingly expressing availability just as George Michael gives up hope
jokes.explanation: Cruel dramatic irony - perfect timing to torture George Michael with what he can't have

jokes.text: God, why am I the only one who ever comes through in this family?
jokes.setup: Only Lucille shows up to support George Sr.
jokes.punchline: Claiming to be the reliable family member while everyone else actually has legitimate reasons for absence
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille self-aggrandizement despite being equally dysfunctional

jokes.text: Call it what you want. I'm tired of paying Lupe to clean one dish.
jokes.setup: George Sr. moved by her declaration of love
jokes.punchline: Reveals her real motivation is domestic convenience, not love
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - deflates romantic sentiment with purely practical concern

jokes.text: Oh, for God's sake. He's on his own for two days, and he joins a gang.
jokes.setup: George Sr. appearing with prison inmates
jokes.punchline: Lucille's interpretation of normal prison social dynamics as gang membership
jokes.explanation: Great Lucille observation treating prison friendship like teenager rebellion

jokes.text: These guys? Oh, I've got them for the afternoon. Besides, they're the only thing I have left to live for in this world.
jokes.setup: Lucille commenting on his 'gang'
jokes.punchline: George Sr. treating hardened criminals like a rental and claiming they're his only joy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about prison relationships and George Sr.'s dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: That's right... dull, nothing-going-on-upstairs... Michael.
jokes.setup: Lucille revealing Marta's interest in Michael
jokes.punchline: Her backhanded description of her own son as boring and stupid
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille casual cruelty toward her children disguised as exposition

jokes.text: Is that my belt?
jokes.setup: Michael and Gob fighting physically
jokes.punchline: Lucille's focus on her accessory during family violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille priorities - material possessions over family welfare

jokes.text: George Sr., having never heard his charges... listed consecutively in one sitting, panicked and ran with great intensity.
jokes.setup: George Sr. suddenly fleeing courtroom
jokes.punchline: Narrator's matter-of-fact explanation that hearing all charges at once caused panic
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan explanation for George Sr.'s dramatic overreaction to his own crimes

jokes.text: Stop it before someone gets hit in the face!
jokes.setup: Michael and Gob fighting while Buster wants to experience violence
jokes.punchline: Buster trying to prevent the exact thing he's been begging for
jokes.explanation: Ironic moment showing Buster's conflicted desires about violence

jokes.text: That too.
jokes.setup: Lindsay suggesting they remove more clothing
jokes.punchline: Tobias agreeing to remove something else (likely still not pants)
jokes.explanation: Continues never-nude visual gag with minimal dialogue

jokes.text: Hit me in the face! Hit me in the face! Hit me in the face! Please! Hit me in the face!
jokes.setup: Brothers fighting while Buster desperately wants to experience violence
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate begging to be punched during actual fight
jokes.explanation: Escalates Buster's earlier desire into desperate pleading during actual violence

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I'm not totally sure who you are.
jokes.setup: Buster asking what about him after Marta breaks up with both brothers
jokes.punchline: Marta's honest admission she doesn't know who this person is
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Buster's attempt to insert himself into romantic drama

jokes.text: That's what it feels like to get punched in the face.
jokes.setup: Buster's long quest to experience violence
jokes.punchline: Finally gets punched (by woman) and calmly observes the sensation
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to running gag - Buster finally gets his wish and treats it like scientific observation

jokes.text: This is all moot, because she never got a chance to know me.
jokes.setup: Brothers arguing about who Marta preferred
jokes.punchline: Michael's arrogant assumption that knowing him would have been decisive
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's ego despite being equally rejected as Gob

jokes.text: Well, it's effective. Yeah, it really is.
jokes.setup: Buster finally getting punched after asking for it all episode
jokes.punchline: Brothers' matter-of-fact assessment of punching effectiveness
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat treating violence with casual professional assessment

jokes.text: I slept very well last night.
jokes.setup: Tobias overcompensating after intimate moment with Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly announcing his good sleep to prove normalcy
jokes.explanation: Shows Tobias's inability to handle intimacy naturally, even in aftermath
